title: cbi_plus dataset metadata sidecar (dataset_meta.json)
description: One dataset_meta.json per dataset folder. It declares units and semantics machine-readably so loaders convert from DECLARED facts, never from tribal knowledge. Born from two critical incidents: km/h ingested as mph (silent garbage rankings) and a per-lane vs total-flow contract contradiction (capacities off by the lane count). See docs/ISSUE_REGISTER.md SIM-M1 / SIM-T1.

timezone.description: 'local_naive' (timestamps are local clock, no tz) | an IANA zone the timestamps are IN (must be converted to local naive before diagnose) | 'utc'

speed.description: unit of the speed column AS SHIPPED

flow_scope.description: THE trap: package contract is per_lane; loaders divide when total_all_lanes

loader.description: which cbi_pipeline loader consumes this dataset (custom_script = see files.notes / the benchmark's reproduce_*.py)

files.description: role -> relative path (roles: states, chain, network, sensor_info, readings, notes...)

columns.description: per-column notes for anything non-obvious: {column: {unit, description}}

imputation_flag_column.description: column marking imputed cells (e.g. is_observed); imputed cells must NEVER calibrate

confidence_columns.description: e.g. INRIX confidence_score / cvalue — low-confidence rows are reference fill, not measurements

lane_tags_reliable.description: false = derive effective lanes from data (p99 flow / 1900), never trust the tag

convention.description: how upstream->downstream ordering is derived (e.g. 'milepost ascending', 'chain row order — milepost DECREASES downstream on S/W corridors')
